case_name: Faizan Farooqui and Farhat Farooqui, Individually and on Behalf of the Estate of Azra Farhat (D) v. BRFHH Shreveport, LLC, Brijesh M. Patel, M.D., Edwin W. Herron, M.D., and the State of Louisiana

summary.brief: Faizan Farooqui and Farhat Farooqui, Individually and on Behalf of the Estate of Azra Farhat (D) v. BRFHH Shreveport, LLC, Brijesh M. Patel, M.D., Edwin W. Herron, M.D., and the State of Louisiana. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Ms. Azra Farhat presented at the hospital complaining of chest pain; she was admitted to the hospital, and approximately 6 days later underwent coronary bypass surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Her heart stopped beating and could not be revived, and thus she died.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Brown testified affirmatively to the existence of a chance of a better outcome, i.e., a 'possible better outcome.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Brown

evidence_index.text: Dr. Brown’s testimony also indicates that the recommendation of the American College of Surgeons for Ms. Farhat’s situation, regardless of the Plavix in her system, would have been to perform surgery without delay.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Brown

evidence_index.text: This writ application is DENIED and the judgment of the trial court denying the defense’s MSJ is AFFIRMED.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: While any chance of a better outcome is compensable, such a chance still must be proved by a preponderance of the evidence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ms. Azra Farhat presented at the hospital complaining of chest pain and was admitted. Approximately 6 days later, she underwent coronary bypass surgery.

focused_exam.cardiovascular: Recurrent angina post-MI, normal LV pump

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Coronary artery disease with recurrent angina post-MI

requestables.name: Cardiology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation to assess the need for urgent coronary bypass surgery

reveal.result_summary: Recurrent angina post-MI, known triple-vessel coronary disease, normal LV pump
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates urgent need for coronary bypass surgery

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to hospital with chest pain

timeline_phases.name: Pre-Surgical Workup
timeline_phases.description: Assessment and decision-making regarding timing of surgery

key_events.event: Decision to delay surgery due to Plavix

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient admitted with recurrent angina post-MI
decision_points.prompt: When should the coronary bypass surgery be performed?

options.clinical_reasoning: Urgent surgery recommended due to recurrent angina and known triple-vessel disease

options.description: Delay surgery to allow Plavix to clear
options.clinical_reasoning: Delayed due to Plavix, despite urgent indications

actual_action_defendant.description: Surgery delayed to allow Plavix to clear
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant delayed surgery due to Plavix

expected_action_court.description: Surgery should have been performed urgently
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: American College of Surgeons recommendation for urgent surgery

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in surgery despite urgent indications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Urgent surgery should have been performed
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Lost chance of a better outcome

patient_outcome.description: Patient's heart stopped during surgery and could not be revived

key_findings: Breach of duty in delaying surgery
Causation established - delay cost a chance of a better outcome

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Urgent surgery could have improved outcome

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with chest pain and is admitted

tests_performed.result: Recurrent angina post-MI, known triple-vessel coronary disease

diagnoses.diagnosis: Coronary artery disease with recurrent angina

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by delaying surgery
court_findings.legal_significance: Lost chance of a better outcome
